generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance IncubatorHeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Precision-controlled heating element embedded in the styrofoam incubator bed, regulated via closed-loop feedback with DS18S20 sensors for temperature maintenance within ±0.1°C tolerance"
]

instance IncubatorFanActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Air circulation system operating at 12V/24V, synchronized with heatbed to maintain uniform thermal distribution within the styrofoam enclosure"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance IncubatorTopTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer deployed at the top of the styrofoam incubator box, measuring temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TopTemperatureData
]

instance IncubatorMiddleTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned in the middle of the incubator box, measuring temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MiddleTemperatureData
]

instance IncubatorBottomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer located at the bottom of the styrofoam incubator box, measuring temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BottomTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TopTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IncubatorTopTemperatureSensor
]

instance MiddleTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IncubatorMiddleTemperatureSensor
]

instance BottomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IncubatorBottomTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical representation of thermal dynamics using differential equations to model heat transfer within the styrofoam incubator enclosure"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RaspberryPiProcessingUnit, PythonScipyPackage
]

instance StateEstimationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter implementation for state estimation of temperature dynamics in the incubator system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyPackage
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous high-frequency temperature data stream (real-time) collected from all three DS18S20 sensors via 1-Wire bus"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TopTemperatureData, MiddleTemperatureData, BottomTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RaspberryPiProcessingUnit, RabbitMQMessageBroker
]

instance CalibrationParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares calibration parameters derived from sensor data alignment with model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RaspberryPiProcessingUnit, PythonScipyPackage
]

instance PerformanceMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time performance metrics (safety/optimality) derived from model predictions and sensor validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TableauDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiProcessingUnit : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded computing unit on Raspberry Pi handling real-time data processing, actuator control, and algorithmic implementations for the incubator DT"
    DTDFVocab:enables IncubatorControlService, TemperatureMonitoringService
]

instance PythonScipyPackage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scientific Python package implementing mathematical modeling (differential equations), algorithmic implementations (Kalman filters), and calibration algorithms for the DT"
    DTDFVocab:enables ThermalModel, StateEstimationModel
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized message broker running on Raspberry Pi facilitating low-latency, decoupled data streaming from sensors to DT components"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataStream
]

instance TableauDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization platform providing aggregated metrics (temperature profiles, energy usage) for operator monitoring and trend analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables IncubatorPerformanceDashboard
]

instance SupervisoryControllerFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rule-based or model-predictive controller framework implementing PID loops for closed-loop adjustments to maintain operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:enables IncubatorControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance IncubatorControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment service for maintaining incubator temperature/humidity within ±0.1°C/±5% tolerances using supervisory controllers"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureAdjustmentAction, FanAdjustmentAction
]

instance TemperatureMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time monitoring service assessing system performance (safety/optimality) via model predictions and sensor validation"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceMetricsInsight, SafetyViolationInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Algorithm-driven fault detection service identifying performance anomalies and cause-effect relationships (e.g., force dynamics)"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance IncubatorPerformanceDashboard : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization service presenting aggregated metrics (temperature profiles, energy usage) to operators via Tableau dashboards"
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardVisualization
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance PerformanceMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time performance metrics derived from model predictions and sensor validation, including safety/optimality assessments"
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts generated when system performance deviates from operational requirements (e.g., temperature thresholds crossed)"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Cause-effect relationships identified through algorithmic processing (e.g., force dynamics in temperature variations)"
]

instance TemperatureAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of heatbed/fan actuators to maintain target temperature ±0.1°C"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FanAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of air circulation to maintain uniform temperature distribution"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains IncubatorHeatBedActuator, IncubatorFanActuator,
                 IncubatorTopTemperatureSensor, IncubatorMiddleTemperatureSensor,
                 IncubatorBottomTemperatureSensor, ThermalModel
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is implemented via a **bang-bang controller** on the Raspberry Pi, which listens for commands through the **RabbitMQ server** for real-time adjustments. Command messages are transmitted as **text-based commands** (not JSON or other formats) without explicit authentication or validation mechanisms. Feedback from the physical system is implicitly handled by the controller’s response to detected violations (e.g., safety/optimality deviations), though no explicit confirmation mechanism or priority-based handling is described. The controller directly influences the physical twin without conflict resolution protocols or command execution logging.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system operates with **discrete, time-triggered synchronization** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its digital counterpart, relying on **periodic data collection** from sensors over time. While the documents do not specify exact synchronization frequencies, the DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with the best parameter estimates derived from collected data. The temporal granularity is implied to be **event-driven or batch-based**, as the DT integrates models, monitors performance via violations (e.g., safety/optimality checks), and triggers interventions (human or algorithmic) only upon detected deviations. No mention of real-time, continuous, or low-latency pipelines is provided; instead, the system operates in a **discrete, iterative feedback loop** where data is processed in cycles to inform model updates and decision-making."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system follows a **centralized architecture pattern**, where a unified framework integrates **modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimisation technologies** to enhance the Physical Twin (PT). The core components include:
  - **Data streams** collected from sensors over time,
  - **Models** representing both cyber and physical aspects of the PT and its environment,
  - **Algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators, optimisers) that process data and models to generate insights or control actions.
  The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively refine models based on real-time data from the PT, ensuring alignment between digital and physical behaviors. When violations (e.g., safety or performance deviations) are detected, the system triggers **human or algorithmic interventions** to adjust the PT. The orchestration relies on a **closed-loop feedback mechanism**, where decisions are made dynamically to maintain optimal or safe operation. The documents do not specify explicit orchestration tools or patterns but imply a **service-oriented integration** of these components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle encompasses phases where models evolve from conceptual design to operational deployment and continuous refinement. Initially, during **development**, the DT integrates **abstract representations** (e.g., CAD models) with **dynamic models** (e.g., differential equations for physical behavior) to capture specific aspects of the Physical Twin (PT) while excluding non-critical details. This phase emphasizes **modeling different system aspects** (cyber-physical and environmental) to enable **optimization, cause-effect analysis, and scenario measurement** before deployment.

    Upon deployment, the DT transitions into **operational monitoring**, where **real-time data** (collected via sensors) continuously updates the DT’s models through **calibration algorithms** to refine parameter estimates. This phase supports **safety and optimal performance monitoring**, with violations triggering **automated or human-driven adjustments** to the PT. The lifecycle governance involves **integrating models persistently** into the DT framework rather than discarding them post-deployment, ensuring long-term adaptability and value retention.

    Transition management relies on **algorithm-driven synchronization** between the DT and PT, ensuring consistency between simulated and real-world behavior. No explicit lifecycle stages (e.g., phases like 'conception', 'implementation', 'maintenance') or governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards) are detailed in the documents, but the emphasis on **continuous calibration and model integration** implies iterative refinement.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validation**:
  The DT’s fidelity is ensured through **separate geometric and behavioral models**, each addressing distinct aspects of the system (e.g., CAD vs. differential equations). Validation/verification (V&V) methods are implied to involve **calibration algorithms** that update model parameters using real-time PT data, reducing uncertainty. Error sources include discrepancies between PT and DT (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations), though no quantification methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) are mentioned. **Quality assurance** is implicit in the DT’s iterative feedback loop: monitors detect deviations from optimal/safe performance, triggering corrections via algorithms or human intervention. The document does not specify formal metrics (e.g., RMSE) or automated error-detection mechanisms, but emphasizes the DT’s **continuous validation** against PT behavior.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** relies on a **single-node, point-to-point communication** via the **RabbitMQ server running on the Raspberry Pi itself**. Sensors communicate over the **1-Wire bus** (not Wi-Fi/Bluetooth), ensuring low-latency, low-power data exchange. The system lacks explicit mention of AMQP protocol details, but the RabbitMQ broker facilitates **real-time, bidirectional messaging** between the physical and virtual components. No security protocols (e.g., TLS, encryption) or redundancy mechanisms are specified, implying a **non-redundant, on-premise setup** with minimal network overhead.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin deployment** is a **single-node, on-premise system hosted exclusively on the Raspberry Pi**, utilizing its embedded computational resources for real-time processing. No cloud, edge, or distributed deployment models are referenced. Computational requirements are implied to be **low-power and lightweight**, relying on the Pi’s hardware for model execution, data storage, and algorithmic operations. Storage is not explicitly detailed beyond sensor data retention (likely temporary, given the context), and no scalability mechanisms (e.g., load balancing) or reliability assurances (e.g., automated restarts) are mentioned. The deployment model is **static**, with no mention of dynamic updates or failover mechanisms.
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    The provided documents do not mention any **industry standards**, **specifications**, **certification requirements**, or **interoperability standards** for Digital Twin systems. No frameworks (e.g., ISO 22400 for DTs) or protocols (e.g., OPC UA for data exchange) are referenced.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) framework integrates **calibration algorithms** to dynamically update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment using real-time sensor data from the Physical Twin (PT). This process ensures that the DT's predictive and analytical capabilities remain accurate under varying operating conditions. Within the DT structure, **monitors** are employed to continuously assess the system's performance for safety and optimality. These monitors operate by comparing predicted behavior (derived from calibrated models) against actual PT measurements, triggering alerts or interventions when deviations exceed predefined thresholds. While the document does not explicitly mention cybersecurity protocols, authentication, or access control mechanisms, the DT inherently necessitates safeguards to ensure data integrity and system reliability. The inclusion of a **safe protocol** is explicitly referenced as being incorporated into the low-level driver layer, implying a foundational safety mechanism to prevent unauthorized or unsafe interactions with the PT."
]
